title: Clarification on applicability of requirement for resident director.
description: Clarification on applicability of requirement for resident director.

text: ceneral Clrcular No. 2Sl2O14 No. I/22/|3-CL,V Government of India Ministry of Corporate Affairs sth Floor, A Wing, Shastri Bhavan, Dr R.P. Road, New Delhi Dated :- 266 June. 2O14 To All Regional Directors, All Registrars of Companies, All Stakeholders. Subject: Clarllicatlon on appllcability of requirement for resldent dltector. Sir, _ Section 149(3) of the Companies Act, 2013 (Act) requires every company to have at least one director who has stayed in India for a total period oi no1 less than 182 days in the previous calendar year. Government has received requests from stakeholders for clarification with regard to applicability of these provisions in the current calendar./financia1 year. 2. The matter has been examined. It is clarified that the ,residencv requirement' would be reckoned from the date of commencement of section 14i of the Act i.e. 1$ April, 2014, Tlne frrst ,previous calendar year, for compliance with these provisions would, therefore, be Calendar year 2014. The period to be taken into account for compliance with these provisions will be the remaining period of calendar year 2014 li.e. lst April to 31"1 December). Therefore, on a proportionate basis, the number of davs for which the director(s) would need to be resident in India. during Calendar year.2014, shall exceed 136 days. 3. Regarding newly incorporated companies it is cladfied that companies incorporated between 1.4.2014 to 3O.9.2O14 should have a resident director either at the incorporation stage itself or within six months of their incorporation. Companies incorporated aJter 30.9.2014 need to have the resident director from the date of incorporation itself. This issues with the approval of the competent auttrority.
